title: Carrot vs. Stick: Goal-Seeking and Avoidance

runtime.expected_outcome: A C with positive logits on 'goal' and negative logits on 'wall-hit' produces a clean, wall-avoidant path. Remove the penalty and the agent bumps into walls.

audiences.kid: Give the agent a reward at the goal and a sting when it bumps a wall; it learns both lessons at once.
audiences.real: Positive C entries attract; negative C entries repel. The agent's plan optimises for both simultaneously without any extra reward machinery.
audiences.equation: Pragmatic(pi) = -E_q[ln p(o)]. Negative logits blow up -ln p(o) on avoided observations, driving the agent away.
audiences.derivation: Avoidance emerges without a separate reward function because the C vector is already log-space. Penalties are negative log-probabilities, just another constraint on the generative model.

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate attractive + aversive preferences encoded in a single C vector.
orchestrate.role: Preference-engineering tutor extending the C-vector basics.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns; c_preference_override sets a high goal logit and a strong wall-hit penalty.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
